Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Its Motion To Reclassify The Matter From Limited To Unlimited; Reopen Discovery; And Strike The Nonbinding Arbitration_1106061-01
Off Calendar Nov-13-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT BARRON'S JEWELERS Motion To Reclassify The Matter From Limited To Unlimited; Reopen Discovery; And Strike The Nonbinding Arbitration Award is granted. While the Cross-Complaint is not entirely clear, the clerk should have automatically re-classified the case upon the filing of the Cross-Complaint under Leonard v. Sup. Crt. (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 34, 43 ("The motion to reclassify was unnecessary because upon the clerk filing the first amended cross-complaint on April 11, 2014, (given that counsel offered to pay the filing fee but his offer was refused), the case should have been classified as unlimited."). The Cross-Complaint is an unlimited jurisdiction cross-complaint. It refers to "Superior Court" and "the damages'exceed the minimum jurisdictional limits of the Superior Court." (Cross-complaint, 2:21-22). The case should have also never been sent to judicial arbitration because defendant is seeking more than $50,000. Discovery shall remain open because it was closed only due to the erroneous reference of this case to judicial arbitration. The motion to strike is moot because defendant filed a trial de novo to vacate the judicial arbitration award. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
